Tool_pension_commutation_Description: Commute up to 40% pension for lump sum. Official commutation table, 15-year restoration, break-even.
Tool_pension_commutation_Subtitle: Pension × % → lump sum vs reduced pension
Tool_pension_commutation_LearnText: Commutation lets you take a portion of pension as a one-time lump sum. Enter monthly pension, commutation % (max 40%), and a commutation factor (years of purchase). The tool shows lump sum, reduced monthly pension, and a simple break-even.
Tool_pension_commutation_FormulaNote: Simplified educational model. Official factors vary by age and service rules; restoration after ~15 years is not modelled here.
Tool_pension_commutation_PercentHint: Typically up to 40% of pension can be commuted.
Tool_pension_commutation_FactorHint: Years of purchase — ~9.81 is a common illustrative age-60 factor.
Tool_pension_commutation_Step_4: See lump sum, reduced pension, and break-even.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Abbreviati: Pension commutation — lump sum vs reduced pension
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Purpose: Estimate lump sum and reduced pension when commuting a share of pension.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_WhyKnow: Commutation trades lifelong pension for cash now — break-even helps decide.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_RealLife: Central/state pensioners choosing commutation at retirement.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Goal_2: See lump sum vs reduced pension
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Nps_Wh: For contribution and corpus planning before annuity.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Annuit: For pension from a retirement corpus.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Tax_Wh: For tax on pension / lump sum context.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Differenti: FinYatra shows lump sum, reduced pension, and break-even in one view.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Motto: Cash now vs pension forever — know the trade-off.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Title: Home Loan Tax Benefit Calculator Section 24(b)
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Description: ₹2L + 80C ₹1.5L deductions. Old vs new regime, joint loan benefit up to ₹7L.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_LearnText: Under the old regime, Section 24(b) allows interest deduction (₹2L cap if self-occupied) and Section 80C allows principal repayment up to ₹1.5L. Enter first-year interest and principal, occupancy, regime, and slab to estimate tax saving.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_FormulaNote: Educational. New regime generally disallows these deductions. Joint-loan higher limits are not auto-modelled — enter your share of interest/principal.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_RegimeHint: New regime: 24(b)/80C home-loan benefits typically not available (simplified).
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_ScenarioSubti: Self-occupied vs let-out vs new regime
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_ExampleStep_1: Interest ₹2,50,000; principal ₹1,80,000; self-occupied; old regime; 30% slab.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Purpose: Estimate old-regime tax saving from home-loan interest and principal.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_WhyKnow: Self-occupied interest is capped; let-out interest is often fully deductible (simplified).
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_RealLife: First home loan, comparing old vs new regime, joint ownership share.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_Home: To derive EMI and first-year split from loan terms.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_80c_: To plan the full ₹1.5L basket including ELSS/PPF.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_Tax_: For overall old vs new regime compare.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Differen: FinYatra isolates 24(b) and 80C with old vs new regime contrast.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Motto: Interest and principal — two deductions, one home loan.
Tool_ltcg_Description: Long-term capital gains at 12.5%. Equity ₹1.25L exempt, property indexation choice, Section 54/54EC.
Tool_ltcg_LearnText: Long-term capital gains (LTCG) apply after the holding threshold — 12 months for equity, 24 months for property (simplified). Equity gets a ₹1.25L exemption then 12.5%; property is taxed at 12.5% with optional 54/54EC exemption.
Tool_ltcg_FormulaNote: Illustrative post-Budget rates. Indexation for property is not auto-applied here — enter indexed cost as buy price if needed.
Tool_ltcg_ExemptionHint: Optional amount that reduces taxable LTCG.
Tool_ltcg_Result_BelowThreshold: Holding below LTCG threshold — LTCG math shown for reference
Tool_ltcg_Step_2: Enter buy, sell, and holding months.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Purpose: Estimate long-term capital gains tax on equity or property.
Tool_ltcg_Info_WhyKnow: Holding period and the ₹1.25L equity exemption change the tax bill.
Tool_ltcg_Info_RealLife: Selling stocks after a year, property sale with 54EC bonds.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Alt_Cg_When: When you need STCG and LTCG in one tool.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Alt_Elss_When: For tax-saving equity with LTCG on exit.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra focuses on LTCG with equity exemption and 54/54EC inputs.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Motto: Hold long enough — then tax the gain, not the whole sale.
Tool_stcg_Description: Short-term capital gains. Equity 20% (Budget 2024), property at slab rate. Wait vs sell analysis.
Tool_stcg_LearnText: Short-term capital gains (STCG) apply before the long-term threshold. Listed equity STCG is often 20%; property STCG is taxed at your slab. Compare selling now vs waiting for LTCG.
Tool_stcg_FormulaNote: Illustrative rates. Waiting assumes the same sell price — markets can move.
Tool_stcg_HoldingHint: STCG if equity < 12 months or property < 24 months.
Tool_stcg_ScenarioSubtitle: STCG today vs LTCG after threshold
Tool_stcg_ExampleStep_4: If waited for LTCG: exempt ₹1.25L, taxable ₹25,000 × 12.5%.
Tool_stcg_ExampleStep_5: Compare tax saved by waiting (price assumed unchanged).
Tool_stcg_Step_2: Enter buy, sell, holding, and slab.
Tool_stcg_Step_3: See STCG tax at 20% or slab.
Tool_stcg_Info_Purpose: Estimate short-term capital gains tax and wait-vs-sell impact.
Tool_stcg_Info_WhyKnow: A few months of holding can change equity tax from 20% STCG to 12.5% LTCG.
Tool_stcg_Info_RealLife: Booking profits early, property flip, year-end tax planning.
Tool_stcg_Info_Alt_Cg_When: For both STCG and LTCG together.
Tool_stcg_Info_Alt_Elss_When: For lock-in equity that exits as LTCG.
Tool_stcg_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra highlights sell-now STCG vs wait-for-LTCG side by side.
Tool_stcg_Info_Motto: Sometimes waiting a few months is the cheapest tax tip.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Description: Offset gains with losses. No wash sale rule in India. STCL/LTCL strategy, year-end planning.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_LearnText: Tax-loss harvesting uses realised losses to offset gains. Simplified India rules: STCL can set off STCG then LTCG; LTCL can set off only LTCG. Unused losses may be carried forward (shown as remaining).
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_FormulaNote: Educational set-off order. India has no US-style wash-sale rule, but other conditions apply. Confirm with a CA.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_ExampleStep_1: STCL ₹1.5L offsets STCG ₹2L → net STCG ₹50k; STCL left = 0.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_ExampleStep_4: Compare with tax if no losses were booked.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Step_4: See net gains and tax saved.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Purpose: Estimate how realised losses reduce taxable capital gains.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_WhyKnow: Year-end harvesting can cut tax without changing long-term allocation much.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_RealLife: Booking losers before 31 Mar, rebalancing with tax awareness.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Goal_3: See tax saved vs no harvest
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Differenti: FinYatra applies simplified STCL/LTCL set-off and shows tax saved clearly.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Motto: Harvest losses with rules — not randomly.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Description: Quarterly schedule 15 Jun/Sep/Dec/Mar. 234C penalty calculator, presumptive single instalment.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_LearnText: Advance tax is paid in instalments: 15% by 15 Jun, 45% by 15 Sep, 75% by 15 Dec, and 100% by 15 Mar. Enter estimated tax and amount already paid to see remaining tax, shortfall for the current milestone, and the full calendar.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_FormulaNote: Educational schedule. 234C interest on shortfall is not computed here — use the interest/penalty tool for that. Presumptive taxpayers may have different timing.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_PaidHint: Advance tax + TDS credited toward liability (simplified).
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_CalendarSubtit: Cumulative % and amount due by each date
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_ExampleStep_5: Use the calendar for Jun/Sep/Dec/Mar milestones.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Step_1: Enter estimated tax and amount paid.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Abbreviat: Advance tax due dates — quarterly calendar
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Purpose: Show how much advance tax is due by each quarterly date.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_WhyKnow: Missing a milestone can attract 234C interest.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_RealLife: Self-employed quarterly payments, freelancers tracking TDS + advance.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Goal_1: Enter estimated tax and paid amount
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Goal_2: See shortfall for current due date
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Alt_Tds_W: When TDS already covers part of tax.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Alt_Advan: For installment amount focus with same schedule.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Different: FinYatra centres the due-date calendar with remaining-to-milestone amounts.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Motto: Know the date — then pay the percent.
